Patch Set 5:

well... I'm still getting many messages like this:

/home/alexey/head1/libcfs/libcfs/workitem.c: In function ‘cfs_wi_sched_create’:
/home/alexey/head1/libcfs/libcfs/workitem.c:384:37: error: ‘%02d’ directive outp
ut may be truncated writing between 2 and 11 bytes into a region of size between
 0 and 15 [-Werror=format-truncation=]
    snprintf(name, sizeof(name), "%s_%02d_%02d",
                                     ^~~~
/home/alexey/head1/libcfs/libcfs/workitem.c:384:4: note: ‘snprintf’ output betwe
en 7 and 39 bytes into a destination of size 16
    snprintf(name, sizeof(name), "%s_%02d_%02d",
    ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      sched->ws_name, sched->ws_cpt,
      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      sched->ws_nthreads);
      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Patch Set 5:

IOW, I can't build :)

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(4 comments)

Patch Set 5:

> How many? Shouldn't we be fixing them instead of masking them?
12, AFAICS

in some cases I'm not sure what to do. for example:

/home/alexey/head1/lustre/mgs/mgs_llog.c:2784:17: note: ‘snprintf’ output between 6 and 69 bytes into a destination of size 40
                 snprintf(mti->mti_uuid, sizeof(mti->mti_uuid),
                 ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
                          "%s_UUID", mti->mti_svname);
                          ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

what's wrong with truncation here? isn't it C anymore?

Patch Set 5:

> in some cases I'm not sure what to do. for example:
>
> /home/alexey/head1/lustre/mgs/mgs_llog.c:2784:17: note: ‘snprintf’ output between 6 and 69 bytes into a destination of size 40
>                 snprintf(mti->mti_uuid, sizeof(mti->mti_uuid),
>                 ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
>                          "%s_UUID", mti->mti_svname);
>                         ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

We should check for truncation and return an error in that case. The warning is only emitted by gcc in cases where the return value of snprintf() is unused.

Patch Set 6:

> We should check for truncation and return an error in that case. The warning is only emitted by gcc in cases where the return value of snprintf() is unused.

sorry, I don't understand this.. I find this rule strange at least. for example, in cfs_wi_sched_create() we form a string to be used as a process name, which has nothing to do with correctness. this isn't an error at all to have that string truncated. w can increase buffer from 16 to 28, but what's the point? just to increase stack footprint?

Patch Set 6:

> sorry, I don't understand this.. I find this rule strange at least. for example, in cfs_wi_sched_create() we form a string to be used as a process name, which has nothing to do with correctness. this isn't an error at all to have that string truncated. w can increase buffer from 16 to 28, but what's the point? just to increase stack footprint?

Yes, I agree that there will be false positives or and cases where the truncation is harmless. But my point is that *overall* it's better to have warning enabled than not.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 6:

> Yes, I agree that there will be false positives or and cases where the truncation is harmless. But my point is that *overall* it's better to have warning enabled than not.

ah, before this specific case I was in absolute agreement with this statement :)
actually I don't mind to fix at least some of them, but in few cases I'm not sure how to do exactly.

Patch Set 6:

> actually I don't mind to fix at least some of them, but in few cases I'm not sure how to do exactly.

What happens if the return value of snprintf() is explicitly cast to void?

(Sorry, I would check myself, I do not have a system with gcc7 installed right now.)

parents.subject: LU-10465 lov: increase default stripe size to 4MB

commit.subject: LU-9934 build: support for gcc7
commit.message:
LU-9934 build: support for gcc7

Supress few false warnings with a compiler option when
building the lustre kernel modules. A few other fixes
to make lustre buildable on Fedora.

Change-Id: If14d226e5d92ae9ce54e216d032df94d9398654e
Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/28810
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
